title: Reamker: The Cambodian Ramayana
description: Cambodia's national epic: the story of Rama adapted through Khmer Buddhism and carved into the walls of Angkor Wat.
introduction: The Reamker (Glory of Rama) is Cambodia's adaptation of the Indian Ramayana, transformed through centuries of Khmer Buddhist culture. The story is carved in bas-relief across 50 meters of gallery at Angkor Wat — the largest religious monument ever built. The Khmer version emphasizes Buddhist themes of karma and compassion absent from the Hindu original, and its characters have become the archetypal figures of Cambodian classical dance.

translation.original_text: ព្រះរាមបានជាស្ដេចមួយពិសេស គឺជាព្រះនារាយណ៍ដែលចុះមកលើផែនដី។
translation.text: Preah Ream was no ordinary king — he was Narayana who descended to earth.

translation.original_text: សោកសៅកើតឡើងនៅក្នុងព្រៃ។ ព្រះរាមធ្វើដំណើរ ដើម្បីរក្សាធម៌។
translation.text: Sorrow arose in the forest. Preah Ream journeyed forth to uphold the dharma.
